prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: Sure! One common misconception that many people have is about the effectiveness of multitasking. Many believe that multitasking helps them get more done and be more efficient. However, research has shown that multitasking can actually decrease productivity and increase the likelihood of errors. When you switch between tasks, your brain has to readjust and refocus, which can take time and mental energy. So, if you think you're being more efficient by multitasking, you might be wrong about its true impact on your productivity.
